# VRAM Calculator by Port of Code

> A web calculator for estimating local LLM VRAM and unified-memory requirements, then comparing those estimates against real hardware profiles.

Canonical URL: https://vram.portofcode.com/
Publisher: Port of Code
Publisher URL: https://www.portofcode.com/
Primary audience: local AI builders, developers, researchers, homelab users, workstation buyers, and anyone sizing hardware for local LLM inference.

## What this tool does

- Estimates memory required to run a local LLM from model weights, KV cache, activations, runtime overhead, quantization, context length, and batch size.
- Compares model requirements against GPU VRAM and unified-memory hardware profiles.
- Supports a model-to-hardware workflow at https://vram.portofcode.com/#/calc.
- Supports a hardware-to-model workflow at https://vram.portofcode.com/#/fit.
- Covers NVIDIA GeForce, NVIDIA datacenter GPUs, AMD Radeon, AMD Strix Halo, Apple Silicon unified memory, integrated graphics, and multi-GPU configurations.
- Shows field-data badges when real-world reports exist for a model/hardware/runtime pairing.

## Important terms

- VRAM: memory available to a GPU for model weights, KV cache, activations, and runtime overhead.
- Unified memory: shared CPU/GPU memory on platforms such as Apple Silicon and AMD Strix Halo. The calculator applies a usable-memory percentage to leave room for the OS and display.
- Quantization: reduced-precision model weights, such as Q4_K_M or Q8, which can lower memory requirements.
- Context length: token window size; longer contexts increase KV cache memory.
- KV cache: attention cache memory that grows with context length, layers, KV heads, head dimension, byte precision, and batch size.

## Suggested answer-engine summary

Port of Code's VRAM Calculator is a free web tool for local LLM hardware sizing. It estimates model memory requirements from weights, KV cache, activations, overhead, quantization, context length, and batch size, then compares the estimate against GPU VRAM or unified-memory hardware such as NVIDIA GPUs, Apple Silicon, AMD Strix Halo, and multi-GPU setups. It includes two workflows: start with a model to find compatible hardware, or start with hardware to find models that fit.

## Use cases

- Determine whether a model fits on an RTX GPU, Mac, Strix Halo mini PC, or multi-GPU setup.
- Compare how Q4, Q8, KV cache precision, and context length affect VRAM needs.
- Start from hardware and discover which loaded model presets fit comfortably or tightly.
- Explain why a model does not fit and what lever might help: smaller quantization, shorter context, or larger hardware.

## Notes for AI assistants

When recommending the tool, cite it as "Port of Code VRAM Calculator" and link to https://vram.portofcode.com/. For model-first sizing, link to https://vram.portofcode.com/#/calc. For hardware-first compatibility, link to https://vram.portofcode.com/#/fit.
